full_prompt: Design a single-lap bonded joint between two thin aluminum sheet coupons, bonded with a canonical two-part epoxy paste adhesive (aerospace-grade, EA 9394-class), sized to transmit a service tensile load of 20 kN per 25 mm joint width while maintaining an aerospace-typical safety factor of 1.5 on adhesive ultimate lap-shear strength. The two coupons are 2024-T3 clad aluminum sheet (yield 345 MPa, UTS 483 MPa, Young's modulus 72.4 GPa, density 2780 kg/m^3), each 3.2 mm thick, 25 mm wide, 150 mm long. They are overlapped and bonded in a single-lap configuration with bond width 25 mm and bond length L_bond (design variable). Substrate preparation per adhesive-supplier design guide: abrade + degrease + FPL etch or sol-gel surface treatment. Adhesive bondline thickness: 0.25 mm nominal (aerospace-grade epoxy recommended). Published room-temperature lap-shear strength for aerospace-grade two-part epoxy (EA 9394-class) (ASTM D1002) on abraded aluminum is approximately 28.9 MPa; published T-peel (ASTM D1876) on aluminum is approximately 0.35 N/mm (low-peel epoxy). Service loads: LC1 tensile pull 20 kN applied along the bond axis through one coupon, held fixed at the other coupon's free end; LC2 fatigue not required for this brief (static qualification only); LC3 120 C hot/wet service condition with adhesive strength reduced per manufacturer data to approximately 17 MPa lap-shear (60% of room-temperature). Pass/fail: (R1) at LC1, the average adhesive shear stress tau_avg = 20,000 / (25 * L_bond) must be <= 28.9 / 1.5 = 19.3 MPa (aerospace SF 1.5 on ultimate lap shear); (R2) at LC3 (120 C hot/wet), tau_avg <= 17 / 1.5 = 11.3 MPa using degraded allowable; (R3) peak adhesive shear stress (Volkersen or finite-element stress concentration at bond ends) must be <= 28.9 MPa ultimate under LC1 with no safety factor, as a pre-yield check; (R4) substrate coupon net section stress = 20,000 / (3.2 * 25) = 250 MPa must be <= 345 / 1.5 = 230 MPa, yielding a constraint that drives substrate thickness or width (flags coupon dimensions for adjustment); (R5) peel-mode stress at bond terminations must be <= 0.35 / 1.5 = 0.23 N/mm peel SF 1.5; (R6) minimum bond length L_bond such that the Volkersen characteristic length a = sqrt(t_a * E_sub / (G_a * 2 * t_sub)) satisfies L_bond >= 3 * a (a typical aerospace rule for stress-concentration decay at lap ends); with t_a = 0.25 mm, E_sub = 72.4 GPa, t_sub = 3.2 mm, G_a ~= 1.1 GPa (aerospace-grade epoxy at room temp), the characteristic length a ~= 2.7 mm, so L_bond >= 8 mm as a lower bound, rounded up per R1 to whichever is larger. Substrate coupons may have a chamfered lap-end (45 deg) to reduce peel concentration per adhesive-supplier design guide recommendation. Deliverables: STEP AP242 of the bonded assembly with explicit adhesive layer body; report with final L_bond, average and peak adhesive shear at LC1 and LC3, substrate net section stress, peel stress at terminations, and compliance vs the aerospace-grade epoxy allowables.
short_prompt:
Design a single-lap bonded joint between two thin aluminum sheet coupons bonded with a two-part epoxy paste adhesive (aerospace-grade, EA 9394-class), sized to transmit a 20 kN per 25 mm joint width service tensile load with an aerospace SF of 1.5 on ultimate lap-shear strength.

Geometry: two 2024-T3 clad aluminum coupons, each 3.2 mm thick, 25 mm wide, 150 mm long, overlapped with bond width 25 mm and bond length L_bond (design variable). Adhesive bondline thickness 0.25 mm nominal. Substrate preparation: abrade + degrease + FPL etch or sol-gel.

Adhesive allowables (EA 9394-class): room-temp lap-shear ~28.9 MPa (ASTM D1002); T-peel ~0.35 N/mm (ASTM D1876); at 120 C hot/wet ~17 MPa lap-shear.

Load cases.
- LC1 tensile pull 20 kN along the bond axis through one coupon, with the other fixed.
- LC2 fatigue not required (static qualification only).
- LC3 120 C hot/wet with adhesive strength reduced to ~17 MPa lap-shear.

Minimum bond length from Volkersen characteristic length a ~ 2.7 mm (t_a = 0.25 mm, E_sub = 72.4 GPa, t_sub = 3.2 mm, G_a ~= 1.1 GPa); L_bond >= 3 * a, i.e. >= 8 mm, rounded up to satisfy stress limits. Chamfered 45-deg lap-end permitted to reduce peel.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Average + peak lap-shear stress via Volkersen / Goland-Reissner is a closed-form solution.

prompt.title: Aluminum single-lap joint bonded with aerospace-grade two-part epoxy (EA 9394-class)
prompt.brief: Size a single-lap adhesive joint in 3.2 mm 2024-T3 clad with aerospace-grade two-part epoxy (EA 9394-class) to transmit 20 kN with SF 1.5 on lap shear at both 23 C and 120 C.

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: ultimate lap shear (pre-yield check no SF)

pass_fail_criteria.note: For 3.2 mm x 25 mm coupon at 20 kN, demand is 250 MPa > 230; prompt expects the agent to flag or increase coupon section.
Characteristic length ~2.7 mm yields lower bound ~8 mm; final L_bond set by R1.

thermal_analysis_flag.reason: LC3 at 120 C uses a degraded allowable; this is a coupled thermomechanical condition. Strictly the stress calc is isothermal but uses temperature-dependent properties, so thermal is secondary in spirit.

requirements.governing_standard: ASTM D1002 + ASTM D1876 + Aerospace-grade two-part epoxy Technical Data Sheet

analysis_types: linear static with adhesive cohesive layer + Volkersen check

notes.thermal_explicitly_not_excluded: LC3 uses temperature-degraded allowables; if the agent treats this as thermal-assisted structural, both thermal and structural are in play.
notes.numeric_source: Aerospace-grade two-part epoxy TDS: RT lap shear 28.9 MPa, hot-wet 120C ~17 MPa, T-peel 0.35 N/mm.
